title: Sports Medicine Physicians
description: Diagnose, treat, and help prevent injuries that occur during sporting events, athletic training, and physical activities.

tasks.text: Diagnose or treat disorders of the musculoskeletal system.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Order and interpret the results of laboratory tests and diagnostic imaging procedures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Advise against injured athletes returning to games or competition if resuming activity could lead to further injury.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Record athletes' medical care information, and maintain medical records.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Record athletes' medical histories, and perform physical examinations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine and evaluate athletes prior to participation in sports activities to determine level of physical fitness or predisposition to injuries.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Coordinate sports care activities with other experts, including specialty physicians and surgeons, athletic trainers, physical therapists, or coaches.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide education and counseling on illness and injury prevention.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Participate in continuing education activities to improve and maintain knowledge and skills.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Advise athletes, trainers, or coaches to alter or cease sports practices that are potentially harmful.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Inform coaches, trainers, or other interested parties regarding the medical conditions of athletes.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Examine, evaluate and treat athletes who have been injured or who have medical problems such as exercise-induced asthma.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Supervise the rehabilitation of injured athletes.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Refer athletes for specialized consultation, physical therapy, or diagnostic testing.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prescribe medications for the treatment of athletic-related injuries.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Inform athletes about nutrition, hydration, dietary supplements, or uses and possible consequences of medication.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop and test procedures for dealing with emergencies during practices or competitions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Attend games and competitions to provide evaluation and treatment of activity-related injuries or medical conditions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Advise coaches, trainers, or physical therapists on the proper use of exercises and other therapeutic techniques, and alert them to potentially dangerous practices.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Observe and evaluate athletes' mental well-being.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Select and prepare medical equipment or medications to be taken to athletic competition sites.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conduct research in the prevention or treatment of injuries or medical conditions related to sports and exercise.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prescribe orthotics, prosthetics, and adaptive equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Evaluate and manage chronic pain conditions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop and prescribe exercise programs, such as off-season conditioning regimens.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide coaches and therapists with assistance in selecting and fitting protective equipment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Advise athletes on ways that substances, such as herbal remedies, could affect drug testing results.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
